title: Orion Knowledge Base Entity
description: Universal schema for all entity types in the Orion LLM knowledge base. Shared base structure with type-specific extensions discriminated by entity_type. Designed for weak LLM extraction: simple fields, closed enums, deterministic IDs.

entity_id.description: Deterministic globally unique identifier. Format: orion:{entity_type}:{normalized_key}. Never changes once assigned.

entity_type.description: Discriminator that selects type-specific required fields.

name.description: Canonical display name. Title case. Trademark symbols stripped.

aliases.description: Alternative names, abbreviations, internal codenames. Used for dedup matching.

description.description: 2-4 sentence factual description. Must include: what it is, one boundary or constraint. No marketing language.

owner.description: entity_id of the product or platform this entity belongs to. Required for all types except product.

relationships.description: Typed directed edges to other entities. Maximum 15 per entity.

source_document.description: Document(s) this entity was extracted from. Comma-separated if multiple sources.

id_strategy.description: Rules for generating deterministic entity_id from entity name. Applied by pipeline, not by JSON Schema validation.
steps: Replace spaces, hyphens, slashes, ampersands with underscore
Remove all other special characters (parentheses, dots, commas, colons)
id_strategy.stability: Once assigned, entity_id NEVER changes even if name is corrected. Corrections go in aliases.

product_type.description: Coarse classification of what kind of product this is.

orion_tier.description: Position in the Orion platform hierarchy.

primary_domain.description: Primary clinical or technical domain. E.g., cardiac_rhythm_management.

primary_users.description: Primary user categories. E.g., clinicians, engineering_teams.

user_visibility.description: How visible this product is to end users.

purpose.description: 2-3 sentences using causal language: why this product exists. Start with the problem it solves.

what_breaks_without.description: Consequences if this product did not exist. 1-3 sentences.

responsibilities.description: What this component owns. One responsibility per item. Copy from source bullets.

non_responsibilities.description: What this component explicitly does NOT own. Critical for anti-hallucination.

depends_on.description: entity_ids of components or capabilities this component requires.

why_exists.description: 1-2 sentences explaining why this component is needed.

responsibility_class.description: One sentence: what class of problems this platform service solves.

what_it_owns.description: Capabilities this domain is responsible for.

consumed_by.description: entity_ids of products that consume this capability.

enforcement_model.description: How products interact with this capability. E.g., 'Products submit context, receive allow/deny, enforce locally.'

trigger.description: What initiates this workflow. One sentence.

steps.description: Ordered sequence of workflow steps. One step per item. Preserve source order.

completion_condition.description: What defines successful completion of this workflow.

safety_gates.description: Safety or validation checks that must pass during this workflow.

failure_handling.description: What happens when this workflow fails at any step.

invariants.description: Conditions that are always true during this workflow.

decision_statement.description: What was decided. One clear sentence.

rationale.description: Why this decision was made. One reason per item.

tradeoffs_accepted.description: What was sacrificed or accepted as cost. One tradeoff per item.

rejection_reason.description: Why it was rejected. One sentence.

constraint_type.description: Category of constraint. boundary=hard limit, non_goal=intentionally excluded, limitation=structural restriction, regulatory=compliance-driven.

what_is_prevented.description: Explicit list of things this constraint prevents. One item per 'does NOT' statement.

why_it_exists.description: 1-2 sentences explaining the rationale for this constraint.

contrast_with.description: How another product handles this differently. Optional.

concept_type.description: What kind of data concept. entity=modeled object, metric=measured signal, lifecycle_state=state machine position.

represents.description: What real-world thing this concept models. One sentence.

key_attributes.description: Important conceptual properties of this data concept.

data_constraints.description: Rules and invariants governing this data. E.g., 'immutable after session end'.

lifecycle_states.description: Valid lifecycle states this data moves through. E.g., transient, session_bound, persisted, archived, disposed.

problem_domain.description: Problem category. E.g., security, architecture, scalability, data_flow, governance, identity.

why_hard.description: What makes this problem difficult. One reason per item.

consequences_without.description: What goes wrong if this problem is not solved. One consequence per item.

how_solved.description: Approach taken to address this problem. One approach per item.

type.description: depends_on=A requires B. enables=A makes B possible. constrains=A limits B. owns=A contains B. solves=A addresses problem B. uses=A consumes B. contrasts_with=A and B are deliberately different. rejects=decision A rejected alternative B.

context.description: One sentence explaining why this relationship exists.
